error_context.description: Returned errors should preserve sentinel identity while adding operation, key/path, and boundary context.

resource_lifecycle.description: I/O resources, transactions, goroutines, timers, and buffers should be closed, rolled back, canceled, or released on every path.

context_cancellation.description: Context-aware APIs should check cancellation, propagate deadlines, and document whether returned readers or writers continue using the context.

concurrency_race.description: Concurrent writes, shared mutable state, and generation counters should be race-free and covered by focused tests.

security_permissions.description: Filesystem and network defaults should fail closed, use narrow permissions, and avoid accidental public exposure.

time_serialization.description: Serialized time values should be deterministic across monotonic clock data, time zones, and parsing formats.

path_portability.description: Path and URI handling should be cross-platform and resistant to traversal or separator ambiguity.

schema_migration.description: Persistent formats should have version checks, forward-compatibility guards, and migration hooks before schema changes accumulate.

test_quality.description: Tests should prove observable behavior with real code where practical and avoid brittle interaction-only mocks.

docs_operational.description: Operational caveats, compatibility limits, and user-visible behavior should be documented near the feature.

performance_allocation.description: Hot paths should avoid avoidable copies, unbounded allocation, and repeated expensive work.
